goal: Move the remaining factory support modules behind the factory namespace while preserving top-level imports through compatibility wrappers.
constraints: Preserve existing import paths for callers and tests
Keep the factory namespace exports coherent as the real home of the factory surface
behavioral_changes: The real methodology and graduation implementations now live under chip_labs.chip_factory.
The old top-level modules now act as compatibility wrappers.
The factory namespace now exports the main methodology and graduation helpers directly.
verification_targets: PYTHONPATH=src python -c "from chip_labs.methodology import get_proven_patterns; from chip_labs.graduation import assess_graduation; from chip_labs.chip_factory.methodology import get_creation_checklist; from chip_labs.chip_factory.graduation import assess_graduation as moved_assess_graduation; from chip_labs.chip_factory import get_patterns_for_area; print('factory-support-move-imports-ok')"
follow_up: At this point the factory surface is mostly behind chip_factory; future work can focus on removing redundant top-level indirection only when safe.
If phase 7C continues, the next likely moves are selective serving internals rather than more factory work.
